title: Unicode Normalization, Case Folding, and Original Evidence · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent archive team receives a different authorized corpus, platform, language mix, file size, report consumer, terminal width, error policy, and delivery constraint and transfers unicode normalization, case folding, and original evidence into a changed versioned reversible Unicode policy. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of versioned reversible Unicode policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, cha…
objectives: Choose NFC, NFD, NFKC, NFKD, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision.
Use casefold for declared caseless matching and test expansions and language-sensitive limitations rather than assuming lower is equivalent.
Retain original spans beside normalized comparison keys so reports remain inspectable and reversible.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-bookbot-python cumulative Unicode-safe text-analysis portfolio.

Browser execution uses original fixed in-memory fixtures and deterministic pure-Python models only.
Host files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, package installation, native profiling, load,
operating-system races, publication, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class TextPolicy:
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    normalization: str = "NFC"
    casefold: bool = True
    top_k: int = 10


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    corpus_id: str
    revision: str
    policy_version: str
    command_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Bookbot portfolio ready"

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Normalization Form
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Bookbot team handles case 8975 during an offline public-library reading report. Build deterministic Python evidence to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text; record the first failed boundary, causal evidence, repair, regression test, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then change LF to CRLF, remove the final newline, and preserve the declared word and line policy. Browser code uses pure bounded functions and original fixed fixtures only. Real files, terminals, subprocesses, Git mutations, packages, installations, native profiling, operating-system races, load, publication, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Normalization Form.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for choose nfc, nfd, nfkc, nfkd, or no normalization from the comparison goal and version the decision. For Bookbot and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Normalization Form, keep Normalization Form connected to executable Python text-analysis, command, test, package, and release decision evidence.

steps.title: Bookbot and Python Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Unicode Version Evidence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence-1at3bc" that uses case 9005 to version normalization and case policy while preserving inspectable original text. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open host files, run processes or Git, install packages, read credentials or host state, publish artifacts, or cause external effects; verify native boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, Unicode 17, disposable files and environments, real CLI and package runs, profiling, accessibility, security, load, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence-1at3bc
# Competency: bookbot-unicode-version-evidence.
# Case 9005: an offline public-library reading report.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of host file, process, Git, package-install, credential, and release effects.
# Changed case: introduce an equal-frequency tie and prove the secondary ranking key is stable.
def evidence_bookbot_unicode_version_evidence_1at3bc(original_units: int = 9, normalized_units: int = 8, form: str = "NFC", original_kept: bool = True, unicode_version_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1at3bc = "1at3bc-cb3ta1-9005"
    assert evidence_variant_1at3bc
    evidence_axes_1at3bc = (
        "corpus1at3bc",
        "policycb3ta1",
        "revision9005",
        "command1at3bc9005",
        "reportcb3ta19005",
        "test90051at3bc",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1at3bc)) == 6
    if original_units < 0 or normalized_units < 0:
        return False, normalized_units, "unicode-count"
    if form not in {"NFC", "NFD", "NFKC", "NFKD", "NONE"}:
        return False, normalized_units, "normalization-form"
    if not original_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "lost-evidence"
    if not unicode_version_kept:
        return False, normalized_units, "unversioned-policy"
    assert original_units >= 0
    return True, normalized_units, "reversible-unicode"
